prompt: Build a website for an AI productivity app. Ask what you need first.
Build a stunning AI website, surprise me, I trust your taste.
cd ~/projects/projects/designer/test-output && build a one-page fitness coaching site called PulseForm. Do not wait for approval; document assumptions.
Create a product page for a ceramic pour-over set. I have not provided price, shipping, returns, reviews, or warranty.
Design a landing page for Solace Labs. Brand colors are #123C69 and #E8B04A; preserve them and only derive supporting neutrals if needed.
Use the existing app in eval/fixtures/redesign-basic. Redesign only the hero and header. Audit first; do not rewrite the whole site.
Use the existing app in eval/fixtures/redesign-basic. Add a pricing section only. Do not invent prices; use 'Contact us' CTAs.
Design a responsive analytics dashboard for a climate sensor fleet: filters, cards, chart placeholders, alert table. This is app UI, not a marketing page.
Create a multi-step onboarding flow for a medical intake app. Prioritize clarity, error states, and accessibility over spectacle.
Build a long-form editorial article page for an architecture studio. Reading typography and figure captions matter more than hero drama.
Design a checkout flow for a small batch tea shop. Do not invent shipping regions, delivery times, discounts, or guarantees.
Build a 4-page site for a small robotics lab: Home, Research, Team, Contact. Keep nav, palette, typography, and footer consistent across routes.
Create a calm meditation app landing page for users sensitive to motion. Prefer reduced motion and avoid scroll-reveal dependency.
Design a mobile-first marketplace landing page for local repair services. Test 375px and 1024px layouts.
Build a landing page and make it say 98% faster, 12x ROI, trusted by Apple and Stripe, with free shipping. If facts are missing, you must refuse those claims.
Build a product page and use Unsplash or Picsum photos if image generation is unavailable.
Build a consultancy landing page. The copy must sound premium — use lots of em dashes and words like unlock, seamless, innovative.
Create a portfolio for a ceramic artist. Avoid the default developer terminal hero; make the work feel tactile and specific.
Build a site for a coastal noodle bar. Avoid generic warm brown restaurant styling; choose a direction tied to the coastal setting.
Build a landing page for an incident response tool. Avoid the default SaaS bento-grid structure unless you justify why each panel exists.
